pred_reason: The contract exhibits integer overflow in getTokens function (line ~212) without proper bounds checking, directly aligning with SWC-101's arithmetic issues classification. This vulnerability allows potential DoS and incorrect data retrieval through manipulated inputs. Evidence: Integer overflow in getTokens function at line ~212; No overflow protection for start + count calculation; Static analysis confirmed controlled-array-length vulnerability

findings.Issue: Missing Access Control in registerMintedToken Function
findings.Description: The registerMintedToken function lacks the onlyOwner modifier, allowing any external address to register tokens. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized token registration, potential registry pollution, phishing attacks, and compromised registry integrity

findings.Description: The getTokens function performs arithmetic operations without overflow protection for start + count calculation. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Potential denial of service, incorrect data retrieval, and possible out-of-bounds array access
findings.Location: TokenRegistryImpl.getTokens() function (line ~212) and static analysis controlled-array-length finding

findings.Issue: Array Index Underflow in unregisterToken Function
findings.Description: The unregisterToken function lacks proper bounds checking for array manipulation, potentially causing underflow. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential contract state corruption, denial of service, and inconsistent token registry data

findings.Description: The Claimable contract's two-step ownership transfer lacks validation for pending owner capability and timeout mechanism. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential permanent ownership lock if transferred to inaccessible address, rendering admin functions unusable

findings.Description: Contract has payable fallback but no withdrawal mechanism, potentially locking ether sent accidentally. This appears in static analysis results only.
findings.Impact: Permanent loss of any ether sent to the contract address
findings.Location: Static analysis locked-ether finding for TokenRegistryImpl contract

findings.Issue: Missing Event Emission in Ownership Transfer
findings.Description: Claimable.transferOwnership should emit an event when updating pendingOwner state. This appears in static analysis results only.
findings.Impact: Reduced transparency and auditability of ownership transfer process
findings.Location: Static analysis events-access finding for Claimable.transferOwnership()
